questions.text: If a person feels that wearing some kind of charm will prevent him
questions.subject: English
questions.explanation: An amulet is an object, typically a small charm or ornament, that is believed to bring luck, protection, or other beneficial effects to its wearer. Amulets are often worn as jewelry or carried on one's person, and they can have cultural, religious, or spiritual significance. They are believed to ward off evil or negative energies and promote good fortune or well-being.

questions.explanation: Research is a systematic process of investigation and inquiry aimed at discovering new knowledge, solving problems, or answering questions. It involves gathering, analyzing, and interpreting data or information to gain insights and understanding on a particular topic or phenomenon. Research can take various forms, including scientific experiments, surveys, literature reviews, observations, and interviews, among others. Its goal is to contribute to the advancement of knowledge and the understanding of the world around us.

questions.explanation: "Profited" refers to the act of gaining a financial or other advantage as a result of a particular action or endeavor. It indicates that someone or something has benefited, either monetarily or in terms of value, from a transaction, investment, or activity. Profit can be measured as the difference between revenue and expenses, or more broadly as any positive outcome or gain.
